---
type: entity
created: 2026-06-15
updated: 2026-06-15
sources: ["[itu-t-l-1480-greening-by-digital](../sources/itu-t-l-1480-greening-by-digital.qmd)"]
tags: [organization]
aliases:
  - "UN"
  - "United Nations"
---


# United Nations

## Basic Information
- Type: organization
- Source: [itu-t-l-1480-greening-by-digital](../sources/itu-t-l-1480-greening-by-digital.qmd)

## Description
The United Nations is the primary international organization under whose mandate the [International Telecommunication Union](international-telecommunication-union.qmd) operates. It provides the global framework, such as the Paris Agreement, which motivates international efforts to limit global temperature rise and achieve Net Zero emissions. The [International Telecommunication Union](international-telecommunication-union.qmd) serves as the specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for telecommunications and ICT matters, effectively linking technical standards to global [Climate Action](../concepts/climate-action.qmd) goals.

## Related Entities
- [International Telecommunication Union](international-telecommunication-union.qmd)

## Related Concepts
None

## Mentions in Source
- "The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) is the United Nations specialized agency in the field of tele­com­mu­ni­ca­tions, information and communication technologies (ICTs)." — [ITU-T L.1480 (Greening by Digital)](../sources/itu-t-l-1480-greening-by-digital.qmd)
